title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 15th July 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: His power-sector pick for a caller investing Rs 1 lakh.
recommendations.detail: The caller had already studied the power sector to some extent and the amount involved was small, so Kutumba Rao named Adani Power on the power side. This sat alongside the anchor's separate suggestion that the caller simply add to the CG Power and Siemens Energy India holdings he already had.

recommendations.note: His automobile pick for the same Rs 1 lakh caller.
recommendations.detail: Named straight away as the buy on the automobile side. Kranthi endorsed it separately as a silent wealth creator worth buying on dips, citing its 7-8 year price chart and its repeated mention at their workshops.

recommendations.note: EV two-wheeler leader; shaping up nicely, can be added.
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said Ather too can be added for the automobile allocation - a good, beautiful two-wheeler electric company. In his market view he called it the leader in EV with no need for any doubt, noting Hero MotoCorp is investing a fresh Rs 1,000 crore on top of its existing ~29% stake, and that the stock is already trading near a record high.

recommendations.note: Under-performing badly among NBFCs; don't focus on a non-performer.
recommendations.detail: A relatively new listing with only about one and a half to two years of history. Results are good with improvement visible from time to time and stable margins around 36-37%, but the stock has fallen from 130 to about 90-92 since the leadership change and is under-performing badly among NBFC stocks. When a stock isn't performing, giving it all your attention is unnecessary when names like Shriram Finance have given wonderful returns. Avoid it.

recommendations.note: His preferred CDMO/CRDMO name; buy on every decline.
recommendations.detail: Asked to name top CDMO/CRDMO companies, he said the choice has become wide, so one must look for the really good outperformer rather than at whether the price is low or high. On a new entry he still prefers a strong performer - Laurus, which has come into CDMO aggressively, on every decline.

recommendations.note: Named among CDMO/CRDMO options.
recommendations.detail: Mentioned as one of the CDMO/CRDMO names worth naming alongside Laurus, without a firm entry call. He referenced Senores separately as one of the new-age companies where interest is visible.

recommendations.note: Named among CDMO/CRDMO options.
recommendations.detail: Named as another CDMO/CRDMO company in the same answer - many names can be given in the space - but with no firm entry level or action attached.

recommendations.note: CDMO name that has risen well recently.
recommendations.detail: Named in the CDMO/CRDMO answer as a stock that has risen well recently. No firm entry call was given.

recommendations.note: Will emerge as a larger NBFC without any doubt.
recommendations.detail: For a viewer holding 120 shares at 480, after good results and positive management commentary. Kutumba Rao said to accumulate more - the stock will emerge as a larger NBFC without any doubt.

recommendations.note: Not performing and not cheap (PE 100+), but Reliance's backing means patience pays.
recommendations.detail: For an SIP investor averaged at 354 and sitting on a 30% loss. It isn't performing at all, and even at current levels it is not a cheap stock with a consolidated PE of 100-plus. Only Reliance's backing is there - but because of that backing it will definitely perform again at some point. Many people's patience has already been tested and many have booked losses; with patience, holding will definitely give returns.

recommendations.note: Start accumulating after the correction; good returns may come.
recommendations.detail: Asked by a Hyderabad viewer whether to buy after the recent correction. Having come out of the tariff troubles the stock went to a 52-week high around 1,500, then corrected back below 1,000. Start accumulating now - good returns may come.

recommendations.note: Good stock, but a wrong high-level entry; old high may take 1-2 quarters.
recommendations.detail: The viewer bought at 7,000 after momentum carried the stock to 8,700 following its mention at the Chennai workshop. Kutumba Rao noted many people try to board this running bus in the momentum and end up with a wrong entry point at higher levels. It is a good stock, but coming back to the old high from here may take a little time - one or two quarters.

recommendations.note: Don't add at all; only hold and keep watching the news.
recommendations.detail: For a Hyderabad viewer holding 500 shares at 102 and asking whether to add. The answer was to not add at all - only hold what he has, and keep watching the newsflow around the stake sale, where the Economic Times reads Fairfax as done while the Financial Express puts Emirates NBD in the race.

recommendations.note: Treat as a trading stock: book part profit, trail a stop on the rest.
recommendations.detail: The viewer is at a 25% profit, so he bought well. The stock had corrected a lot from earlier and is now recovering again. Keep it as a trading stock - book part of the profit from time to time and keep the balance with a trailing stop loss.

recommendations.note: Same treatment as AGI Greenpac: part-book profit, trail the rest.
recommendations.detail: Asked about together with AGI Greenpac. Both are stocks that had corrected a lot earlier and are recovering again, so rather than booking out fully, keep them as trading stocks: book part of the profit periodically and hold the balance with a trailing stop loss.

recommendations.note: No need to sell from the IPO; aggressive management, strong order book.
recommendations.detail: For a viewer holding from the IPO and asking whether to sell now: there's no need. Management is going forward with good aggressive plans - the new aerospace acquisitions and the auto-ancillary order book the company already has are all good; a little more patience is needed to grow the profit further. In his market view he flagged the QIP pricing of around 230.79 as the likely benchmark price, and noted that unusually the stock rose 3% at issue time and stayed strong rather than slipping to a discount - strong on both the price chart and the news.

recommendations.note: Good company; entry was at higher levels; can make new highs in 2 quarters.
recommendations.detail: Part of a Chennai viewer's three-stock question. Indraprastha Medical Corporation is a good company - it just seems the entry was made at somewhat higher levels. Definitely a stock that can come back to new highs again in the next two quarters, so hold.

recommendations.note: Good company held from a high entry; hold for a recovery to new highs.
recommendations.detail: Asked together with Indraprastha Medical and Transrail Lighting. Kutumba Rao grouped them as all good ones where the viewer's entry was simply at higher levels, and said they can definitely come back to new highs in the next two quarters. Hold.

recommendations.note: Good company held from a high entry; hold for a recovery to new highs.
recommendations.detail: The third stock in the same Chennai viewer's question (rendered as 'Cram C' in the captions). Treated the same as the other two - a good company entered at higher levels, able to return to new highs within about two quarters. Hold.

recommendations.note: Mylan exit at ~400 sent it up 6-7%; FIIs picked it up - buy every dip.
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao called this the most interesting price action of the session. When it was said that Mylan would exit at a price of around 400, the stock rising 6-7% is very interesting - because almost all the good foreign institutional investors picked that stock up. So Biocon can be a good bet on every decline, for buying.

recommendations.note: Called the top long ago at 6,000/5,000; weak results, no quick recovery.
recommendations.detail: He had said long ago, when the stock was at 6,000 and 5,000, that the best of the times were over and that it was one of the stocks to avoid. Just as expected, the results were weak (almost 12% EBITDA decline) and the stock fell over 5%. Speculative shorts have expectedly built up and further weakness may come. Unless there is some dramatic corporate restructuring, or unless they change their vision, there are no indications the stock will recover very quickly.
